description: Diagnostic decision tree for A2338 (MacBook Pro 13" M1). Follow sequentially from Step 1. Has Touch Bar (DFR), fan, 2x USB-C (no MagSafe). Charger is B379 (63W).
WARNING: A2338 (820-02020) MBP 13" M1 ONLY. NOT MBA (A2337). SVR AON is 36A (not 30A like A2337). SoC is U0600 (not U6000). J5150 solder pads (not J5100 gasket). Has DFR Touch Bar. Has fan.
data_source: Schematic PNGs (pages 22, 25, 28, 32, 37, 57) cross-referenced with saf-diagnostics-2026-03-02.json (35 A2338 devices, 22 with logic board work). Power-on sequence verified against Eason (iphone_cpu_fix) M1 standby circuit reference PDF.

eason_reference.source: M1 series standby circuit and power-on principle, and 20V boost circuit (Eason @ iphone_cpu_fix)
power_on_sequence: 1. PPVBUS_USBC0 5V → CD3217 MOSFET → PPHV_INT0_AONSW → FF200 → PPDCIN_AONSW
2. PPDCIN_AONSW → R5220 → PPDCIN_AON_CHGR_R → U5200 (power). R5215/R5216 → CHGR_AUX_DET → U5200 (detect)
3. U5200 → Q5230/Q5240 → PPBUS_AON (12.6V temp). CHGR_EN_MVR → R5340/U5340 → P3V8AON_PWR_EN → R5710 → U5700
5. PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN → U8100(MPMU) + U8110 comparator. R8100/R8101 → MPMU_SNS_DIV → U8110. When same>opposite, PMU_VDD_HI(3.8V) → U8100 voltage detect
6. U8100 → all MPMU rails. UC100→PP3V3_AON, UC120→PP1V8_AON. U7700 → SPMU rails
7. M1 hard start (Y0600 24MHz + Y8301 32.768KHz + PMU_RESET_L). M1 reads ROM, I2C to CD3217
8. M1 → U8100 → P5VS2_PWR_EN → UC300 → PP5V_S2 (AFTER hard start)
9. CD3217 reads shared ROM (4 SPI lines), PD via CC → charger boosts to 20V

forced_start_bypass.description: Bypass protective isolation to test if fault is pre-SVR or post-SVR.
steps: 1. Connect PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN to 3.8V externally (4.3V if testing with display)
2. Jumper U8110 pins 4 and 5 to force PMU_VDD_HI high
3. Press start button OR connect to charger for 3 seconds

interpretation.forced_start_works: Fault is FRONT END — CD3217, charger IC, fuses, boost circuit. Check Steps 1-2.
interpretation.forced_start_fails: Fault is BACK END — PMU, SoC, clocks, ROM. Check Steps 3+.

ppvaon_fault_l.description: Active-low fault flag from SVR AON controller U5700. If LOW, SVR has detected overcurrent/overvoltage/thermal fault — the SVR itself has a problem. If HIGH, SVR is healthy, look downstream.
ppvaon_fault_l.action: Single probe eliminates entire SVR AON section

pmu_active_ready.description: Goes HIGH only after MPMU (U8100 SERA) completes full startup sequence. If PP3V8_AON present but PMU_ACTIVE_READY stays low, fault is inside PMU, clocks, ROM, or SoC handshake.
pmu_active_ready.action: Skips past power section to compute section

crystal_32khz.description: PMU_REFCLK32K_OUT — MPMU cannot start without this clock. No oscillation = dead crystal = board won't start regardless of power path health.
crystal_32khz.action: 1 second with oscilloscope eliminates entire clock failure category

soc_force_dfu.description: Forces DFU mode bypassing macOS boot. If DFU works but board won't boot normally, entire power delivery and SoC hardware is fine — problem is NAND/firmware.
soc_force_dfu.action: Instantly separates hardware from software faults. No disassembly needed.

phase_balance.description: Individual phase current sense resistors for the 3-phase SVR AON (36A design). Measure voltage drop across each while running. Zero on one while others are equal = dead phase (open FET, open inductor, bad driver).
phase_balance.action: Identifies exact dead phase without removing anything

cd3217_sys_detect.description: Goes high when charger IC recognizes valid power source. 5V at USB-C but SYS_DETECT low = CD3217 fault. No 5V at port = cable/connector/port controller.
cd3217_sys_detect.action: Splits entire input path in one probe

pp5v_s2_bootstrap.description: PP5V_S2 feeds back to PP3V8_AON VDRV after hard start to sustain SVR AON driver voltage. If board starts then dies after ~1 second, hard start works but bootstrap path is broken.

dfr_touch_bar.description: Touch Bar (DFR) enable. If board boots but Touch Bar is dead, check UT700 controller and JT700/JT710 connectors. Liquid damage hotspot on A2338.
dfr_touch_bar.action: Isolates Touch Bar subsystem from main board faults

fan_check.description: Fan power from PP5V_S2 via QE500 MOSFET. If fan not spinning after boot, check JE501 connector and UE501 level shifter. Fan connector area IC failure documented in SAF.
fan_check.action: Quick check of fan power path

unique_findings: BM 940: U8250 IC explosion on logic board — replaced IC + two CD chips
BM 1166: LCD line short (EDP from CPU) — T2 swap fixed it
BM 1114: CPU swap for badly liquid damaged board
BM 1211: CD chips ROM repair + jumper wires for liquid damage

tree.probe: Bulk capacitors near U5200 charger IC output (page 22). C5254/C5255 area. TO SYSTEM output.

no.saf_context: SAF: BM 1016 at 5V/0.023A — liquid damage, CD chips + touch bar connector repair. BM 1234 at 5V — 'macbook not on and working, logic board has liquid damage, repair logic board and charging port'. BM 1199 — 'repair logic board all liquid damage parts three ic and 6 ragiester and keyboard and charging port'. BM 1211 at 20V/0.6A — 'CD chis ROM and some CD chis line make jumper'. CD3217 and charger IC failures are prevalent on A2338.
check_sequence: Check PPOCIN_USBC_AON from USB-C (page 22) — should have 5V or 20V
Check FF200/FF201 fuses (page 55) — if blown, short downstream of fuse
Check CD3217 port controllers: UF400 (ATC0, page 57) and UF500 (ATC1, page 58)
Check SYS_DETECT from battery connector J5150/Q5155 (page 21) — NOTE: J5150 is SOLDERED, can crack
Check charger IC U5200 (353S01525, page 22) gate drives PS5211-PS5219
Check R5275 sense resistor and XW5260 current sense (page 22)
Thermal cam on charger area for shorted FETs

tree.probe: PP3V8_AON output caps near U5700 (page 25). CS751 area. B5700/RS750 protection.

no.diagnosis: SVR AON (U5700) fault — 36A ICC MAX design, 1MHz
no.saf_context: SAF: BM 940 — 'one ic explosion on logic board (u8250) after we replace ic but still same macbook not taking AMM but on and working' then 'two CD chis issue'. U8250 is near PP3V8_AON distribution. BM 1166 at 20V/0.8A — short circuit detected. BM 1173 — liquid damage near fan area (near PP3V8_AON on A2338).
check_sequence: Check PPVAON_PWR_EN (delayed by U5340 circuit, page 23)
Check PPVAON_FAULT_L (page 25) — if low, U5700 reports fault
Check TS5060 temperature sensor (page 25)
Diode mode on PP3V8_AON output caps — check VDDMAIN distribution (page 100)
Check phase outputs R5710/R5711 (page 25-26)
Check 3-phase driver FETs: Q5800 (DRVL1 via L5800), Q5820 (DRVL2 via L5820), Q5840 (DRVL3 via L5840) — each through sense resistors to PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN (per Eason reference)
Thermal cam on U5700 and phase FETs Q5800/Q5820/Q5840 — 36A design runs hotter than A2337's 30A
SHORTCUT: Try Eason forced start bypass — inject 3.8V on PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN + jumper U8110 pins 4+5 + press start. If board boots, fault is upstream of SVR.

tree.probe: UC100 output for PP3V3_AON, UC120 output for PP1V8_AON (page 36)

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON input to LDOs — if missing, fault is upstream at U5700
If PP3V3_AON missing: check UC100 input/output, diode mode on output cap
If PP1V8_AON missing: check UC120 input/output, diode mode on output cap
Thermal cam on LDO area

tree.probe: Slave PMU buck output caps (page 28). U7700 (TML7763_JPC). BUCK4-BUCK14 via R770A-R770F regulators. Large C77xx cap banks.
tree.expect: Multiple buck rails present — PPVDS_S2M_DRAM, PP2V5_AWAKE_NAND, PP0V88_AWAKE5W_NAND etc.

no.saf_context: SAF: BM 1114 at 5V/0.2A — 'very badly liquid damage' required CPU swap. Liquid corrosion on PMU BGA pads is common. BM 1126 — 'repair logic board liquid damage parts and change new battery'.
check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON input to U7700 via PPVDS_VDDCNT_SPMU (page 28)
Check PPVDS_S2 input and XW7700/XW7701 current sense
Check individual BUCK outputs via R770A-R770F regulators
Diode mode across C77xx output cap banks
Thermal cam on U7700 area for hot component
If single buck missing: find downstream short on that rail before replacing PMU

tree.probe: Master PMU buck output caps (page 32). Large input cap banks. DB100/QR100 power button circuit.
tree.expect: Multiple buck rails present — PPVDD_PCPU_AWAKE, PPVDD_GPU_AWAKE, PPVDD_SOC_S1 etc.

no.saf_context: SAF: BM 940 — U8250 IC explosion near MPMU. After replacing U8250 + two CD chips, board worked. Component-level failure near MPMU is documented on A2338.
check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON_MPMU input to U8100 (page 32)

CRITICAL (Eason ref): Check U8110 comparator — PPBUS_AON via R8100/R8101 → MPMU_SNS_DIV → U8110 same-direction input. PP3V8_AON feeds U8110 opposite input. When same > opposite, U8110 outputs PMU_VDD_HI (3.8V) to U8100 as voltage detect. Without PMU_VDD_HI, U8100 will NOT start.
Check Y8301 crystal (32.768KHz) — U8100 needs this clock (per Eason reference)
Check PMU_ONOFF_L — pulled up by PP1V8_AON_MPMU through R8324
Check PMU_ACTIVE_READY output
Check power button circuit DB100/QR100 (page 32)
Check individual buck output caps
Check nearby ICs for explosion/damage — SAF: U8250 explosion is a known failure
Thermal cam on U8100 and surrounding area
If LDO outputs missing (page 34): check individual LDO enables

tree.probe: UC300 (LT8642S) output (page 37) — 5.15V/6.6A/1.5MHz, startup <15ms. LC329 inductor (CRITICAL). UC710 output (page 39).

check_sequence: Check P5VS2_PWR_EN enable signal
Check PP5V2_PGOOD power good signal (page 37)
Check UC300 (LT8642S) input PPBUS_AON, inductor LC329 (CRITICAL), sense XMC332
Check 5VS2 discharge circuit: QC420, RC423/RC422 (page 38)
Check 5VS2_EN turn off delay: DC401, RC401/RC402 (page 38)
If PP3V3_S2 missing: check UC710, P3V3S2_PWR_EN gating (page 38-39)
Diode mode on output caps for shorts

tree.check: SoC power domains (U0600 M1 TML R6A0-B09)
tree.probe: SoC power pages 10-14. PCPU/ECPU/GPU from PMU bucks. DDR/SRAM from sleep bucks.
tree.expect: Multiple voltage domains present. Board should attempt boot.

no.diagnosis: SoC fault, SPI ROM fault, or T2-equivalent issue
no.saf_context: SAF: BM 1166 at 20V/0.8A — 'one LCD line short (EDP from CPU) after T2 swap macbook working fine'. CPU Issue and T2 Chip faults documented. BM 1114 — CPU swap for badly liquid damaged board.
check_sequence: Check SPI NOR flash (page 17)
Check secure element (page 20)
Check SoC ground connections (pages 15-16)
Check NAND/storage power (pages 64-67)
If all power present but no boot: try DFU mode via USB-C
If EDP display line short suspected: check display connector area (page 68)

substeps.probe: UP700 display power sequencer (SLG4AP4365V, page 69), UP800 BEN backlight (page 70), JP600 connector (page 68)
substeps.saf_context: SAF: BM 1051 — 'screen image not coming, has been repair before logic board, found Camera line has one FL610 and make new jumper'. BM 1166 — EDP from CPU line short.

SAF: BM 1016 — 'touch bar connector has liquid damage, we repair new connector'. BM 1209 — 'touch bar connector has liquid damage, we repair new connector and some liquid damage parts'. Touch Bar connector is a liquid damage hotspot on A2338.

substeps.probe: UF400 (ATC0 left rear/debug, page 57), UF500 (ATC1 left front, page 58), connectors (page 59)
substeps.saf_context: SAF: Multiple cases of 'CD chips' repair. BM 1199 — 'three ic and 6 ragiester and keyboard and charging port'. BM 1211 — 'CD chis ROM and some CD chis line make jumper'. BM 1234 — charging port replacement.

substeps.probe: IOX1 UT101 (PCA6416A), IOX2 UT102 (page 81), UT103 RSLOC isolation, JT200 membrane ZIF (page 82), ESD DZT201-DZT206
substeps.saf_context: SAF: 14 of 22 logic board cases = keyboard fault (64%). BM 1261 at 20V/2.5A, BM 1335 at 20V/2.5A — keyboard + liquid damage. Keyboard backlight shared A2289/A2338 part. Keyboard is the DOMINANT fault on A2338.

substeps.probe: JE501 connector (page 52), QE500 NTS4C569N MOSFET, UE501 level shifter, PP5V_S2_TO_FAN
substeps.saf_context: SAF: BM 1173 — 'after repair logic board we found fan sound lowly after we found near fan connector has Two ic has issue'. Fan connector area IC failure documented.

substeps.probe: UR500 CS42L83 codec (page 75), 4 active amps A/B/C/D (pages 76-77), speaker connectors JR800/JR850 (page 78)
substeps.saf_context: SAF: BM 1234 — 'try to repair audio ic other parts logic board but still same no improve on sound. No point to spent more time'. Audio IC on A2338 can be unrepairable.

substeps.probe: UT305 LDO (page 83), UT300 LDO, JT400 connector (page 84), SPI TPAD via UT360
substeps.saf_context: SAF: BM 1335 — 'keyboard has liquid damage and track pad liquid damage'. BM 1239 — 'swap logic board and change new track pad and flex'.

substeps.note: Rasputin is soldered — rework requires BGA skills
M1 NAND paired to SoC. Non-Ocarina VCC discharge QN420 (page 66).
